title: 4 charged in connection with alleged kidnapping, beating near Nantucket park
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Four Nantucket men, aged 21 to 34, face kidnapping and multiple assault charges related to a Thursday attack.
A 28-year-old victim was allegedly beaten, tied up with an extension cord, and injured near Winter Park on Essex Road.
The suspects are scheduled for arraignment on Monday after being released on bail, with video evidence cited in the investigation.

title: 2 arrested at Logan Airport as ICE activity ramps up
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: ICE arrested Maria Rosales and David Ardila at Logan Airport for immigration issues related to DACA and asylum applications.
Rosales was allegedly subject to a 2017 removal order while Ardila reportedly overstayed his visa during a pending asylum decision.
Attorney Todd Pomerleau noted an increase in ICE arrests at Logan Airport, with many detainees having no criminal history.

title: 5 movies to watch after ‘The Odyssey’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' prompts a list of movies with thematic similarities or direct adaptations of Homer's epic.
'Interstellar' and 'Lawrence of Arabia' are recommended for their epic storylines akin to 'The Odyssey.'
Films like 'O Brother, Where Art Thou?' and 'The SpongeBob SquarePants Movie' offer humorous takes on Homer's tale.

title: Formidable Red Sox extend win streak to 11 games with doubleheader sweep of Rays
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox swept the Tampa Bay Rays in a doubleheader, extending their win streak to 11 games.
Game 1 saw Boston shut out the Rays 10-0 with standout performances from Jake Bennett and multiple batters.
In Game 2, the Red Sox overcame an early deficit to win 5-3, improving their season record to 48-48.

title: Chad Tracy offers updates on several injured Red Sox pitchers
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Ranger Suarez may return July 21 from a groin strain, having shown significant recovery progress.
Connelly Early is progressing from left elbow inflammation and has resumed throwing, though no return date is set.
Kutter Crawford, Tanner Houck, and Johan Oviedo are advancing in their recoveries with hopes to pitch this season.

title: 4 Red Sox who rose their stock following Friday’s doubleheader sweep vs. Rays
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit three times, including a home run and RBI, boosting his role as Boston's primary designated hitter.
Caleb Durbin went 5 for 7 in the doubleheader, maintaining his strong performance as one of Boston's effective batters.
Jake Bennett pitched six scoreless innings, aiding bullpen preservation, while Carlos Narvaez hit 3 for 4 with a home run and 3 RBIs.

title: Robert Kraft backs Mike Vrabel after controversial offseason: ‘No one is infallible’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots owner Robert Kraft expressed support for head coach Mike Vrabel after a controversial offseason incident.
Mike Vrabel denied an affair with reporter Dianna Russini, who resigned after an investigation into their conduct.
The Patriots, led by Vrabel, reached the Super Bowl last year and will begin training camp next Saturday.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Sam Neill's "The Hunt for the Wilderpeople" is now streaming on Prime Video and Plex.
The horror film "Obsession" grossed over $400 million and is available on Peacock.
Anya Taylor-Joy stars in "Lucky" on Apple TV, a crime series involving a $10 million heist.

title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin violated New Hampshire's Civil Rights Act in a 2024 bias-motivated assault on a transgender woman.
The court ordered Lufkin to avoid contact with the victim and imposed a $5,000 fine with $4,000 suspended for three years.
Lufkin pleaded guilty to criminal charges, receiving a suspended sentence totaling 18 months for second-degree and simple assault.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Boston Red Sox have a 10-game winning streak, boosting playoff odds to 44.3% according to FanGraphs.
Red Sox chief baseball officer Craig Breslow remains undecided on potential trade deadline moves before Aug. 3.
ESPN reports Boston may pursue a right-handed hitter, possibly taking on a bad contract at the upcoming deadline.
